example: As your morning alarm blares, you muttered to yourself.
Frustrated, you shout, I can't do anything right!
Being caught talking to yourself can feel embarrassing.
Some people even stigmatize this behavior as a sign of mental instability.
A sign of mental instability.
Decades of psychology research show that talking to yourself is normal.
Self-talk refers to the narration inside your head.
Sometimes called inner speech.
Psychologists define self-talk as verbalized thoughts directed towards yourself.
It also includes reflections you have throughout the day.
Our first experiences with self-talk are mostly vocal.
Lev Vygotsky hypothesized that this kind of speech was key to development.
Outward self-talk tends to become internalized.
Self-talk can help motivate you throughout the day.
Self-talk is often spontaneous and done without conscious control.
What areas of the brain are activated during self-talk?
Engaging in self-talk that's instructional or motivational helps focus.
Instructional self-talk increased their concentration and accuracy.
Distanced self-talk is when you talk to yourself as if in conversation with another person.
Self-talk reduces stress during anxiety-inducing tasks.
Excessively negative self-talk can become toxic.
People who ruminate on problems experience more depression.
CBT focuses on regulating the tone of self-talk.
Replace negative thoughts with more compassionate reflections.
That inner voice is a partner you'll be talking to for many years.
Learn about the relationship between self-talk and procrastination.
